context.sellerGoal: Preserve price, protect renewal uplift language, and convert rollout anxiety into a structured implementation offer.
buyerStakeholders: VP Procurement: asks for discount and cash-flow relief
Security Lead: worried about Salesforce integration readiness
General Counsel: inserted renewal uplift redline
CFO: wants Q3 launch certainty before approving

commercialPolicy.discountFloor: Do not exceed 8% without CFO approval.
commercialPolicy.preferredConcession: Quarterly payment terms plus $35k implementation credit.
commercialPolicy.marginRisk: An 18% discount costs about $216k ARR; expected margin leakage is shown as $140k net risk after close-probability lift.

legalPolicy.redFlag: Removing automatic renewal uplift breaks revenue policy.
legalPolicy.safeCounter: Cap renewal uplift at 5%, require 60-day notice, and allow a volume-tier review.

documents.title: CRM account brief
documents.label: sales
documents.summary: Northstar expanded usage 42% in nine months. Executive sponsor wants the Q3 rollout live before peak season.

documents.title: Pricing and approval policy
documents.label: finance
documents.summary: Seller can offer payment timing and services credits. Discounts above 8% require CFO approval and trigger margin review.

documents.title: Renewal clause redline
documents.label: legal
documents.summary: Buyer removed the 5% renewal uplift. Legal policy requires capped uplift language or executive exception.

documents.title: Security implementation signal
documents.label: risk
documents.summary: Security tickets mention Salesforce readiness seven times. This is likely deployment risk, not pure price resistance.

clientLines: We like the platform, but the price is higher than expected. Procurement is asking for 18% off.
Also, legal changed the renewal uplift clause. They said it was standard.
Honestly, security is worried the Salesforce integration will not be ready before our Q3 rollout.
If you can make the rollout less risky and help cash flow, I think we can get this signed.

objection.diagnosis: This is not a true price objection; price is a proxy for rollout and approval risk.
objection.recommended_line: Before we talk discount, what happens if the Salesforce rollout misses Q3? We can protect cash flow with quarterly terms and include implementation credits.
objection.do_not_say: I can take 18% off if that gets this done today.
objection.business_impact: Avoids a $140k net margin leak while raising close probability by shifting the buyer from price anchoring to risk reduction.

legal.diagnosis: The redline looks standard but removes the renewal uplift engine.
legal.recommended_line: We can accept a rollout success plan, but not that renewal redline; I can send capped 5% uplift language with 60-day notice and a volume-tier review.
legal.do_not_say: That legal change is fine if it helps us close.
legal.business_impact: Blocks compounding renewal leakage while giving the buyer procedural fairness and budget predictability.
